summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Repay faces Kubra purchase opposition
articles.description: A major shareholder of the digital bill payment services provider bashed its plan to buy a bill payment peer.

articles.title: Adyen tool speeds business payments
articles.description: Financial operations at global enterprises remain “stubbornly complex,” as treasury teams juggle multiple banking and payment systems, the company said.

articles.title: Bolt lays off roughly 30% of staff in AI push
articles.description: The workforce reduction marks the fintech’s fourth round of cuts since 2022, as it has navigated leadership changes.

articles.title: Why the subscription model should change
articles.description: “The question isn't whether government intervention in subscription cancellation policies is good or bad,” writes one industry executive.

articles.title: Fed mulls FedNow x-border use
articles.description: The Federal Reserve proposed letting participants in the real-time payments system use intermediaries to make cross-border fund transfers.

articles.title: CHIPS network boasts daily payment growth
articles.description: CHIPS network growth underscores its rising use for large-value domestic and cross-border payments, The Clearing House said.

articles.title: FDIC floats stablecoin guidelines, AML revamp
articles.description: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. proposed a rule under the Genius Act for supervising payment stablecoin issuers.

articles.title: How US credit card fees may ebb
articles.description: The Nilson Report’s publisher explained how competition, merchants and court dynamics are influencing the evolution of interchange fees.
